front: What is the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM)?
back: A decentralized, Turing-complete virtual machine that executes smart contract code on the Ethereum network. It is the core runtime environment for all smart contracts and decentralized applications (dApps), ensuring deterministic execution across all nodes.

front: What is the role of the Web3.js library in the Ethereum stack?
back: It is a collection of JavaScript libraries that allow a client-side application to interact with the Ethereum blockchain. It enables reading data from the blockchain, sending transactions, and interacting with smart contracts via a local or remote Ethereum node.

front: What is Gas in the context of Ethereum?
back: Gas is the unit that measures the amount of computational effort required to execute specific operations on the Ethereum network. Every operation (like a transaction or smart contract function call) consumes gas, which is paid for in Ether (ETH) to compensate miners for the resources used.

front: What is the purpose of the Solidity programming language?
back: Solidity is the primary high-level, object-oriented programming language used for writing and implementing smart contracts on the Ethereum blockchain and other compatible platforms. It is statically typed and designed to target the Ethereum Virtual Machine (EVM).

front: What is an Ethereum Client (or Node Client)? Name two popular ones.
back: Software that allows a device to participate in the Ethereum network by storing a copy of the blockchain, validating transactions, and executing smart contracts. Two popular execution clients are Geth (Go-Ethereum) and Nethermind, while popular consensus clients include Prysm and Lighthouse.
